message.body: @GamingEvolutionCentre 32/64 bit is orthogonal to the x86(-64) vs arm64 architectures.  If what you meant was rather x86 and x86-64 Windows, then arm64 Windows is 64-bit just as x86-64, but it's a whole separate processor architecture that needs emulation to run x86/x86-64 things, which makes software compatibility for running x86 stuff on arm limited in some edge cases and makes drivers incompatible (need drivers for the right architecture).
